titleEs: Explicar un NullPointerException en Spring Boot
descriptionEs: Lee el stack trace y explica la causa raiz al ingeniero de guardia.
preTaskContextEn: You are reviewing an incident in a Spring Boot checkout service. The on-call engineer needs a short explanation before opening a bug ticket.
preTaskGlossesJson: [{"term":"stack trace","gloss":"lista de llamadas que muestra donde ocurrio el error"},{"term":"null check","gloss":"validacion para evitar usar un objeto vacio"},{"term":"on-call engineer","gloss":"ingeniero de guardia que atiende incidentes"}]
duringTaskPromptEn:
java.lang.NullPointerException: Cannot invoke "User.getEmail()" because "user" is null
	at com.acme.checkout.NotificationService.sendReceipt(NotificationService.java:42)
	at com.acme.checkout.OrderController.createOrder(OrderController.java:78)
duringTaskInstructionEs: Escribe en ingles 2 o 3 frases: indica la causa raiz, la linea donde ocurre y la primera accion recomendada.
expectedAnswerEn: The error happens in NotificationService.sendReceipt at line 42 because the code calls user.getEmail() when the user object is null. The user probably comes from OrderController.createOrder at line 78. The team should first check the user lookup and add a null check before sending the receipt.
postTaskLanguageFocus: {"grammar":"present simple for technical explanation","discourseMarkers":["because","first"],"keyCollocations":["null check","root cause","line 42"]}
postTaskExplanationEs: Las explicaciones tecnicas claras usan present simple y conectores de causa como because. Tambien conviene nombrar la linea y la accion siguiente con first para priorizar el siguiente paso.

titleEs: Interpretar un ImportError en un CLI de Python
descriptionEs: Resume por que falla el script y que modulo revisar primero.
preTaskContextEn: A teammate is trying to run a deployment helper from the command line. They pasted the error in chat and asked you to explain it in plain English.
preTaskGlossesJson: [{"term":"virtual environment","gloss":"entorno aislado de Python con dependencias propias"},{"term":"module","gloss":"archivo o paquete importable en Python"},{"term":"command line","gloss":"terminal donde se ejecutan comandos"}]
duringTaskPromptEn:
Traceback (most recent call last):
  File "deploy.py", line 4, in <module>
    from utils.secrets import load_secret
ImportError: cannot import name 'load_secret' from 'utils.secrets' (/app/utils/secrets.py)
duringTaskInstructionEs: Escribe en ingles una respuesta de 2 a 4 frases para el companero. Debes explicar la causa probable y la primera revision recomendada.
expectedAnswerEn: The script fails because Python can find utils.secrets, but that module does not export a function called load_secret. The problem is probably in /app/utils/secrets.py or in the import name. I would first check whether the function was renamed or removed in that file.
postTaskLanguageFocus: {"grammar":"present simple with probable cause","discourseMarkers":["because","probably","first"],"keyCollocations":["export a function","import name","check the module"]}
postTaskExplanationEs: En este tipo de error conviene distinguir entre encontrar el modulo y encontrar el nombre importado. Usar probably y first te ayuda a sonar preciso sin afirmar algo que aun no confirmaste.

titleEs: Explicar un TS2322 en TypeScript
descriptionEs: Analiza el error de tipos y propone el ajuste correcto.
preTaskContextEn: You are helping a frontend developer after a refactor to strict null checks. They need a short explanation they can paste into the issue thread.
preTaskGlossesJson: [{"term":"strict null checks","gloss":"modo que obliga a manejar null y undefined explicitamente"},{"term":"type mismatch","gloss":"desacuerdo entre el tipo esperado y el valor real"},{"term":"issue thread","gloss":"hilo de comentarios del ticket"}]
duringTaskPromptEn:
src/components/ProfileCard.tsx:18:9 - error TS2322: Type 'string | null' is not assignable to type 'string'.
  Type 'null' is not assignable to type 'string'.

18   email={user.email}
           ~~~~~~~~~~

The expected type comes from property 'email' which is declared here on type 'ProfileCardProps'
duringTaskInstructionEs: Escribe en ingles 2 o 3 frases. Explica por que TypeScript rechaza el valor y menciona una posible correccion.
expectedAnswerEn: TypeScript rejects this prop because ProfileCard expects email to always be a string, but user.email can also be null. The component contract and the data do not match. We should either provide a fallback string before passing the prop or update the prop type to accept null.
postTaskLanguageFocus: {"grammar":"cause and solution with either or","discourseMarkers":["because","either","or"],"keyCollocations":["prop type","fallback string","accept null"]}
postTaskExplanationEs: Los errores de tipos suelen explicarse comparando expectativa y realidad: expected type versus actual value. Either...or... es util para proponer dos soluciones validas sin alargar demasiado la respuesta.

titleEs: Diagnosticar un hydration mismatch en React
descriptionEs: Explica por que el HTML del servidor no coincide con el del cliente.
preTaskContextEn: The team just enabled server-side rendering for a dashboard page. After deployment, the browser console started showing hydration warnings on first load.
preTaskGlossesJson: [{"term":"hydration","gloss":"proceso en que React conecta el HTML del servidor con el cliente"},{"term":"server-side rendering","gloss":"renderizado inicial hecho por el servidor"},{"term":"warning","gloss":"mensaje de advertencia que no detiene la ejecucion"}]
duringTaskPromptEn:
Warning: Text content did not match. Server: "09:15" Client: "09:16"
    at span
    at ClockWidget (/app/components/ClockWidget.tsx:12)
    at DashboardHeader (/app/components/DashboardHeader.tsx:8)

ClockWidget renders new Date().toLocaleTimeString() during render.
duringTaskInstructionEs: Escribe en ingles una explicacion de 3 o 4 frases. Debes describir la causa tecnica y una alternativa segura para renderizar la hora.
expectedAnswerEn: This hydration mismatch happens because the server and the browser render different time values for ClockWidget. The component calls new Date().toLocaleTimeString() during render, so the text can change between the server response and the client hydration. A safer option is to render a stable placeholder on the server and update the clock inside useEffect after hydration.
postTaskLanguageFocus: {"grammar":"present simple plus so for consequence","discourseMarkers":["because","so","after"],"keyCollocations":["hydration mismatch","stable placeholder","update after hydration"]}
postTaskExplanationEs: Cuando explicas un hydration mismatch conviene nombrar los dos momentos del render: server response y client hydration. Un nivel B2 puede usar consequence language como so y after para conectar causa, efecto y correccion.

titleEs: Analizar una foreign key violation en Postgres
descriptionEs: Describe por que falla el insert y que dato falta validar.
preTaskContextEn: A production job creates invoice rows after a checkout event. One insert failed and the data engineer wants a short root cause note for the incident channel.
preTaskGlossesJson: [{"term":"foreign key","gloss":"restriccion que exige que una referencia exista en otra tabla"},{"term":"constraint","gloss":"regla de integridad definida en la base de datos"},{"term":"incident channel","gloss":"canal donde el equipo sigue el incidente"}]
duringTaskInstructionEs: Escribe en ingles 3 frases. Explica la causa raiz, el dato faltante y una verificacion recomendada antes del insert.
expectedAnswerEn: The insert fails because invoice_items references invoice_id 9182, but that invoice does not exist in the invoices table. In other words, the child row arrives before the parent record is available. We should verify that the invoice creation step completed successfully and validate the foreign key before inserting item rows.
postTaskLanguageFocus: {"grammar":"in other words for reformulation","discourseMarkers":["because","in other words","before"],"keyCollocations":["parent record","child row","foreign key validation"]}
postTaskExplanationEs: En reportes tecnicos es util reformular la causa con in other words para volverla mas clara. Parent record y child row son collocations frecuentes cuando se explica integridad referencial.

titleEs: Resumir reglas de autenticacion REST
descriptionEs: Lee la documentacion y explica como autenticar una solicitud.
preTaskContextEn: You joined a backend squad that integrates with a payments API. A teammate asks you to explain the authentication rules before they build the client.
preTaskGlossesJson: [{"term":"header","gloss":"campo enviado en la solicitud HTTP"},{"term":"Bearer token","gloss":"token de acceso que se envia en Authorization"},{"term":"retry","gloss":"volver a intentar una solicitud"}]
duringTaskPromptEn:
Authentication
All requests must include an Authorization header with a Bearer token.
Example: Authorization: Bearer sk_live_123
Tokens expire after 60 minutes. If the token is expired, the API returns HTTP 401 with code auth_token_expired.
Clients should request a new token from POST /v1/auth/token before retrying the original request.
duringTaskInstructionEs: Escribe en ingles una explicacion breve para un companero: que header debe enviar, cuando expira el token y que endpoint debe usar para renovarlo.
expectedAnswerEn: Every request must send the Authorization header with a Bearer token. The token expires after 60 minutes, and the API returns HTTP 401 with auth_token_expired when that happens. The client should call POST /v1/auth/token to get a new token before retrying the request.
postTaskLanguageFocus: {"grammar":"must for requirements","discourseMarkers":["when that happens","before"],"keyCollocations":["send a header","token expires","retry the request"]}
postTaskExplanationEs: La documentacion tecnica suele resumirse con must para requisitos y con before para indicar secuencia de pasos. Tambien es importante repetir con precision los nombres del header y del endpoint.

titleEs: Explicar un endpoint de creacion en OpenAPI
descriptionEs: Resume parametros, body y respuesta de un endpoint REST.
preTaskContextEn: The support team needs a simple explanation of how to create a bug ticket from another service. They do not want the full OpenAPI file, only the key contract details.
preTaskGlossesJson: [{"term":"request body","gloss":"contenido JSON enviado en la solicitud"},{"term":"required field","gloss":"campo obligatorio para que la API acepte la solicitud"},{"term":"response","gloss":"datos devueltos por el servidor"}]
duringTaskPromptEn:
POST /v1/tickets
Request body:
{
  "title": "string",
  "severity": "LOW | MEDIUM | HIGH",
  "service": "string",
  "ownerEmail": "string"
}
Required fields: title, severity, service
Responses:
201 Created -> { "id": "tk_123", "status": "OPEN" }
400 Bad Request -> validation errors
duringTaskInstructionEs: Escribe en ingles 3 o 4 frases. Debes explicar que hace el endpoint, que campos son obligatorios y que devuelve si todo sale bien.
expectedAnswerEn: This endpoint creates a new ticket. The client sends title, severity, service, and optionally ownerEmail in the request body, but title, severity, and service are required. If the request is valid, the API returns 201 Created with the new ticket id and an OPEN status. If validation fails, it returns 400 Bad Request.
postTaskLanguageFocus: {"grammar":"present simple for API behavior","discourseMarkers":["but","if","optionally"],"keyCollocations":["request body","required field","returns 201 Created"]}
postTaskExplanationEs: Una buena explicacion de API separa accion, entrada y salida. Optionally y required ayudan a distinguir entre campos opcionales y obligatorios sin sonar ambiguo.

titleEs: Resumir una mutacion GraphQL
descriptionEs: Lee el schema y explica como actualizar el estado de una orden.
preTaskContextEn: A frontend engineer is new to GraphQL and needs a quick explanation of a mutation before wiring the UI. You want to translate the schema into simple operational steps.
preTaskGlossesJson: [{"term":"mutation","gloss":"operacion GraphQL que cambia datos"},{"term":"enum","gloss":"tipo con un conjunto cerrado de valores"},{"term":"payload","gloss":"objeto devuelto por la operacion"}]
duringTaskInstructionEs: Escribe en ingles una explicacion de 3 frases. Debes mencionar el nombre de la mutacion, los campos del input y lo que devuelve.
expectedAnswerEn: The mutation is called updateOrderStatus. It requires an input object with orderId and status, and it can also include an optional reason. The server returns the updated order together with the updatedAt timestamp in the payload.
postTaskLanguageFocus: {"grammar":"requires and can include for contracts","discourseMarkers":["and","also"],"keyCollocations":["input object","optional reason","updated payload"]}
postTaskExplanationEs: En GraphQL conviene hablar de input object y payload para sonar natural. Requires y can include permiten resumir restricciones del schema de manera clara y compacta.

titleEs: Explicar un servicio gRPC de inventario
descriptionEs: Interpreta el proto y resume el contrato para otro equipo.
preTaskContextEn: Platform engineering is documenting internal RPCs for teams that usually work with REST. You need to explain one gRPC method in plain English without losing technical precision.
preTaskGlossesJson: [{"term":"protobuf","gloss":"formato usado por gRPC para definir mensajes y servicios"},{"term":"stream","gloss":"flujo continuo de mensajes"},{"term":"deadline","gloss":"tiempo maximo permitido para una llamada"}]
duringTaskInstructionEs: Escribe en ingles 4 frases. Resume que hace el RPC, que datos recibe, que campos entrega y la restriccion de deadline.
expectedAnswerEn: ReserveStock asks the inventory service to reserve a quantity of one SKU in a specific warehouse. The client sends sku, quantity, and warehouse_id in the request. The response tells us whether the reservation was accepted and can include either a reservation_id or a rejection_reason. Clients also need to set a deadline of 1500 milliseconds for this call.
postTaskLanguageFocus: {"grammar":"can include for conditional fields","discourseMarkers":["either","also"],"keyCollocations":["reserve stock","specific warehouse","set a deadline"]}
postTaskExplanationEs: Los contratos RPC suelen describirse como una accion remota con request y response claros. En un nivel B2 ya conviene mencionar restricciones operativas como deadlines junto con la forma del mensaje.

titleEs: Resumir una especificacion de webhook
descriptionEs: Explica el payload y las validaciones necesarias para procesarlo.
preTaskContextEn: The billing team wants to consume a payment_succeeded webhook safely. They need a concise explanation they can convert into implementation notes.
preTaskGlossesJson: [{"term":"signature","gloss":"firma usada para verificar que el emisor es autentico"},{"term":"payload","gloss":"cuerpo JSON enviado por el webhook"},{"term":"idempotency","gloss":"propiedad que evita procesar dos veces el mismo evento"}]
duringTaskPromptEn:
Event: payment.succeeded
Headers:
- X-Signature: HMAC SHA256 of the raw body
- X-Event-Id: unique event id
Payload:
{
  "paymentId": "pay_981",
  "orderId": "ord_44",
  "amount": 12900,
  "currency": "USD",
  "paidAt": "2026-04-07T13:05:11Z"
}
Consumers must verify the signature before parsing the body and ignore duplicated X-Event-Id values.
duringTaskInstructionEs: Escribe en ingles 4 frases. Explica que evento llega, que headers importan y cuales son las dos validaciones obligatorias antes de procesarlo.
expectedAnswerEn: This webhook notifies us when a payment succeeds. The important headers are X-Signature, which contains the HMAC signature of the raw body, and X-Event-Id, which uniquely identifies the event. Before processing the payload, the consumer must verify the signature to confirm authenticity. It must also ignore duplicated X-Event-Id values to keep the handler idempotent.
postTaskLanguageFocus: {"grammar":"must for mandatory validation","discourseMarkers":["before","also"],"keyCollocations":["verify the signature","raw body","keep the handler idempotent"]}
postTaskExplanationEs: Cuando resumes webhooks es importante separar contenido del payload y reglas de seguridad. Verify the signature y keep the handler idempotent son collocations clave para este genero tecnico.

titleEs: Escribir un commit de bug fix
descriptionEs: Convierte una lista de cambios en un mensaje de commit corto y preciso.
preTaskContextEn: You fixed a small backend bug before the daily stand-up. Your tech lead wants a concise commit message in the conventional commits format.
preTaskGlossesJson: [{"term":"commit message","gloss":"resumen corto del cambio guardado en Git"},{"term":"validation","gloss":"revision de datos antes de procesarlos"},{"term":"bug fix","gloss":"correccion de un fallo"}]
duringTaskPromptEn:
Changes made:
- add null validation for user email before sending receipt
- return 404 when user is missing
- update unit tests for NotificationService
duringTaskInstructionEs: Escribe un mensaje de commit en ingles usando conventional commits. Debe tener una sola linea y empezar con feat o fix.
expectedAnswerEn: fix: validate missing user email before sending receipt
postTaskLanguageFocus: {"grammar":"imperative verb in commit messages","discourseMarkers":[],"keyCollocations":["validate missing user","update tests","return 404"]}
postTaskExplanationEs: Los commit messages en conventional commits usan verbo base y una descripcion breve del cambio principal. Conviene enfocarse en el impacto mas importante y no listar todo el diff.

titleEs: Redactar un commit para una nueva feature
descriptionEs: Resume un cambio funcional nuevo en una sola linea.
preTaskContextEn: Your team added a small feature to the dashboard. The feature is visible to users, so the commit should clearly mention the new capability.
preTaskGlossesJson: [{"term":"feature flag","gloss":"mecanismo para activar o desactivar una funcionalidad"},{"term":"dashboard","gloss":"panel principal de la aplicacion"},{"term":"capability","gloss":"funcionalidad que ahora existe"}]
duringTaskPromptEn:
Changes made:
- add export to CSV button on analytics dashboard
- hide button behind analytics_export feature flag
- show success toast after file download starts
duringTaskInstructionEs: Escribe un mensaje de commit en ingles con formato conventional commits. Debe resumir la nueva feature principal, no cada detalle secundario.
expectedAnswerEn: feat: add CSV export for the analytics dashboard
postTaskLanguageFocus: {"grammar":"imperative verb plus object","discourseMarkers":[],"keyCollocations":["CSV export","analytics dashboard","feature flag"]}
postTaskExplanationEs: Para una feature nueva, el sujeto central suele ser la capacidad visible para el usuario. Add CSV export for the analytics dashboard resume el cambio sin meter detalles de implementacion.

titleEs: Escribir un commit de refactor
descriptionEs: Resume un refactor sin cambiar el comportamiento funcional.
preTaskContextEn: You cleaned up duplicated logic in a service layer. Reviewers should understand that the code structure changed, but the observable behavior stayed the same.
preTaskGlossesJson: [{"term":"refactor","gloss":"reestructuracion del codigo sin cambiar su comportamiento externo"},{"term":"shared helper","gloss":"funcion reutilizable para varias partes del codigo"},{"term":"behavior","gloss":"resultado visible de la aplicacion"}]
duringTaskPromptEn:
Changes made:
- extract repeated date parsing logic from InvoiceService and RefundService
- move helper to DateRangeParser
- keep API responses unchanged
duringTaskInstructionEs: Escribe un mensaje de commit en ingles con conventional commits. Debe indicar que es un refactor y mencionar el objetivo principal.
expectedAnswerEn: refactor: extract shared date range parser for billing services
postTaskLanguageFocus: {"grammar":"concise noun phrase after refactor","discourseMarkers":[],"keyCollocations":["shared parser","billing services","extract logic"]}
postTaskExplanationEs: Refactor suele ir seguido de un sintagma nominal que nombra la pieza reorganizada. Shared date range parser expresa bien el cambio estructural sin sugerir una feature nueva.

titleEs: Redactar un commit con breaking change
descriptionEs: Escribe un commit que advierta un cambio incompatible.
preTaskContextEn: The API contract changed and older clients will break if they keep sending the old field. The commit message should make that risk obvious to the release team.
preTaskGlossesJson: [{"term":"breaking change","gloss":"cambio incompatible con clientes anteriores"},{"term":"contract","gloss":"forma esperada de usar una API"},{"term":"deprecated","gloss":"marcado para dejar de usarse"}]
duringTaskPromptEn:
Changes made:
- remove legacy field customerName from POST /v1/orders
- require customerId instead
- delete compatibility mapper used by v1 mobile clients
duringTaskInstructionEs: Escribe un mensaje de commit en ingles. Debe usar el formato conventional commits y dejar claro que hay un cambio incompatible.
expectedAnswerEn: feat!: replace customerName with customerId in order creation API
postTaskLanguageFocus: {"grammar":"exclamation mark for breaking change","discourseMarkers":[],"keyCollocations":["replace field","order creation API","breaking change"]}
postTaskExplanationEs: En conventional commits, el signo ! comunica un breaking change de manera inmediata. Replace customerName with customerId resume la migracion y el impacto contractual en una sola linea.

titleEs: Escribir un commit de revert
descriptionEs: Resume la reversión de un cambio problemático en producción.
preTaskContextEn: A cache optimization increased API latency in production because it locked the main thread. The team is rolling back the change while they investigate a safer approach.
preTaskGlossesJson: [{"term":"revert","gloss":"deshacer un cambio anterior en Git"},{"term":"latency","gloss":"tiempo que tarda una operacion en responder"},{"term":"roll back","gloss":"volver a una version anterior segura"}]
duringTaskPromptEn:
Changes made:
- remove local LRU cache introduced in commit 7ac9e2d
- restore direct Redis reads for session tokens
- keep metrics logs to compare performance after rollback
duringTaskInstructionEs: Escribe un mensaje de commit en ingles. Debe dejar claro que estas revirtiendo una optimizacion y mencionar el motivo principal.
expectedAnswerEn: revert: remove local session cache after latency regression
postTaskLanguageFocus: {"grammar":"revert plus reason","discourseMarkers":["after"],"keyCollocations":["latency regression","session cache","roll back optimization"]}
postTaskExplanationEs: Los reverts funcionan mejor cuando incluyen la razon del rollback. After latency regression comunica contexto operativo, no solo la accion tecnica de deshacer el commit.

titleEs: Redactar una PR de hotfix de login
descriptionEs: Resume contexto, cambios y pruebas de un hotfix pequeño.
preTaskContextEn: You are opening a hotfix PR because users cannot log in from Safari. Reviewers expect a short note that explains the bug and how you verified the fix.
preTaskGlossesJson: [{"term":"hotfix","gloss":"correccion urgente aplicada rapido en produccion"},{"term":"reviewer","gloss":"persona que revisa el codigo antes del merge"},{"term":"verify","gloss":"comprobar que algo funciona"}]
duringTaskPromptEn:
Context:
Safari blocked login when SameSite was not set on the session cookie.
Changes:
- set SameSite=Lax on the session cookie
- add regression test for Safari login flow
Testing:
- manual login test on Safari 17
- existing auth test suite
duringTaskInstructionEs: Escribe en ingles una descripcion de PR de 4 a 6 lineas con secciones cortas: Context, Changes y Testing.
expectedAnswerEn:
Context
Safari blocked login when the session cookie did not set SameSite.

Changes
- Set SameSite=Lax on the session cookie.
- Added a regression test for the Safari login flow.

Testing
- Manually tested login on Safari 17.
- Ran the existing auth test suite.
postTaskLanguageFocus: {"grammar":"past simple in PR summaries","discourseMarkers":["Context","Changes","Testing"],"keyCollocations":["session cookie","regression test","manual login test"]}
postTaskExplanationEs: Las PR descriptions funcionan mejor con secciones previsibles y verbos en pasado para indicar lo implementado. En un hotfix, el lector quiere ver bug, cambio y verificacion sin rodeos.

titleEs: Describir una PR de feature para reportes
descriptionEs: Explica una nueva funcionalidad y como fue validada.
preTaskContextEn: You finished a reporting feature for account managers. The PR description should help reviewers understand user impact, key implementation points, and coverage.
preTaskGlossesJson: [{"term":"user impact","gloss":"efecto visible para quien usa la aplicacion"},{"term":"coverage","gloss":"alcance de las pruebas"},{"term":"reporting feature","gloss":"funcionalidad para generar o ver reportes"}]
duringTaskPromptEn:
Context:
Account managers need to download weekly churn reports without asking data engineering.
Changes:
- add weekly churn report page under /reports/churn
- support CSV export and date range filter
- add backend endpoint GET /api/v1/reports/churn
Testing:
- unit tests for report filter parsing
- manual export test with staging data
duringTaskInstructionEs: Escribe en ingles una descripcion de PR de 5 a 7 lineas con secciones Context, Changes y Testing.
expectedAnswerEn:
Context
Account managers needed a self-service way to download weekly churn reports.

Changes
- Added a new page at /reports/churn.
- Added CSV export and a date range filter.
- Added the backend endpoint GET /api/v1/reports/churn.

Testing
- Added unit tests for report filter parsing.
- Manually tested the export flow with staging data.
postTaskLanguageFocus: {"grammar":"needed and added for before after narrative","discourseMarkers":["Context","Changes","Testing"],"keyCollocations":["self-service reporting","date range filter","staging data"]}
postTaskExplanationEs: Need a before-after story in a feature PR: problema inicial, solucion implementada y validacion. Self-service reporting y staging data son expresiones naturales en este contexto.

titleEs: Resumir una PR de upgrade de dependencias
descriptionEs: Explica un upgrade tecnico con riesgos y pruebas.
preTaskContextEn: The security scanner flagged several outdated dependencies. Your PR updates them and includes a small compatibility fix in the frontend build.
preTaskGlossesJson: [{"term":"dependency upgrade","gloss":"actualizacion de librerias o paquetes usados por el proyecto"},{"term":"compatibility fix","gloss":"ajuste para mantener funcionamiento con una nueva version"},{"term":"security scanner","gloss":"herramienta que detecta vulnerabilidades"}]
duringTaskPromptEn:
Context:
Dependabot reported vulnerabilities in axios 1.6 and vite 7.0.
Changes:
- upgrade axios to 1.8.2
- upgrade vite to 8.0.5
- update one import path in analyticsChart.tsx for Vite 8 compatibility
Testing:
- bun run build
- smoke test on dashboard and reports pages
duringTaskInstructionEs: Escribe en ingles una descripcion de PR de 5 o 6 lineas. Debe mencionar el motivo, los upgrades y las pruebas ejecutadas.
expectedAnswerEn:
Context
Dependabot reported vulnerabilities in axios and Vite.

Changes
- Upgraded axios to 1.8.2 and Vite to 8.0.5.
- Updated one import path in analyticsChart.tsx for Vite 8 compatibility.

Testing
- Ran bun run build.
- Completed smoke tests on the dashboard and reports pages.
postTaskLanguageFocus: {"grammar":"reported and upgraded in past simple","discourseMarkers":["Context","Changes","Testing"],"keyCollocations":["reported vulnerabilities","upgrade dependency","smoke test"]}
postTaskExplanationEs: En un dependency upgrade el lector quiere entender motivo, alcance y validacion. Reported vulnerabilities y smoke tests ayudan a situar el riesgo y la confianza del cambio.

titleEs: Describir una PR de refactor grande
descriptionEs: Resume un refactor con impacto arquitectonico y estrategia de validacion.
preTaskContextEn: The codebase had duplicated billing logic across three services. You extracted a shared application layer and want reviewers to understand the migration scope clearly.
preTaskGlossesJson: [{"term":"migration scope","gloss":"alcance del cambio entre la version antigua y la nueva"},{"term":"application layer","gloss":"capa donde vive la logica del negocio"},{"term":"duplicate logic","gloss":"codigo repetido con el mismo comportamiento"}]
duringTaskPromptEn:
Context:
Billing rules were duplicated in InvoiceService, RefundService, and SubscriptionService.
Changes:
- introduce BillingApplicationService as the single orchestration entry point
- move pricing and tax rules to shared policy classes
- keep public REST endpoints unchanged
Testing:
- migrate existing service tests to the new layer
- add integration test for refund + invoice combined flow
- run regression suite on billing APIs
duringTaskInstructionEs: Escribe en ingles una descripcion de PR de 6 a 8 lineas. Debe comunicar el objetivo arquitectonico y por que el cambio es seguro.
expectedAnswerEn:
Context
Billing rules were duplicated across three services, which made changes hard to maintain.

Changes
- Introduced BillingApplicationService as the single orchestration entry point.
- Moved pricing and tax rules into shared policy classes.
- Kept the public REST endpoints unchanged to limit migration risk.

Testing
- Migrated the existing service tests.
- Added an integration test for the refund and invoice combined flow.
- Ran the billing API regression suite.
postTaskLanguageFocus: {"grammar":"which clauses for consequences","discourseMarkers":["which","to limit","Testing"],"keyCollocations":["single orchestration entry point","shared policy classes","migration risk"]}
postTaskExplanationEs: En una PR de refactor grande importa justificar por que la nueva arquitectura mejora mantenibilidad y por que el riesgo esta contenido. A B2 le conviene usar which y to limit para expresar consecuencia y mitigacion.

titleEs: Redactar una PR de security patch
descriptionEs: Resume un parche de seguridad con cambios defensivos y validacion.
preTaskContextEn: Security reported that uploaded SVG files could execute untrusted scripts when previewed in the browser. The PR introduces a defensive patch before the next release.
preTaskGlossesJson: [{"term":"sanitize","gloss":"limpiar contenido para eliminar elementos peligrosos"},{"term":"untrusted input","gloss":"datos externos que no deben asumirse seguros"},{"term":"preview","gloss":"vista previa de un archivo antes de usarlo"}]
duringTaskPromptEn:
Context:
Uploaded SVG files could keep embedded scripts and event handlers.
Changes:
- sanitize SVG uploads on the backend
- block inline event attributes during preview rendering
- add content-type validation for image uploads
Testing:
- malicious SVG fixture rejected in integration test
- manual preview test with safe and unsafe files
duringTaskInstructionEs: Escribe en ingles una descripcion de PR de 6 lineas aproximadamente. Debe explicar el riesgo y las defensas aplicadas.
expectedAnswerEn:
Context
Uploaded SVG files could keep embedded scripts and inline event handlers, which created an XSS risk during preview.

Changes
- Sanitized SVG uploads on the backend.
- Blocked inline event attributes during preview rendering.
- Added content-type validation for image uploads.

Testing
- Rejected a malicious SVG fixture in an integration test.
- Manually tested preview behavior with safe and unsafe files.
postTaskLanguageFocus: {"grammar":"could plus past simple for risk and mitigation","discourseMarkers":["which","Testing"],"keyCollocations":["XSS risk","sanitize uploads","unsafe files"]}
postTaskExplanationEs: En seguridad, la PR debe nombrar riesgo y defensa sin sonar vaga. XSS risk, sanitize uploads y unsafe files son collocations utiles para explicar el cambio con precision.

titleEs: Sugerir un null check en code review
descriptionEs: Lee un fragmento y escribe una sugerencia amable y concreta.
preTaskContextEn: You are reviewing a small backend change from a junior teammate. The code works in the happy path, but it does not check whether the customer exists.
preTaskGlossesJson: [{"term":"happy path","gloss":"caso ideal donde todo sale bien"},{"term":"null check","gloss":"validacion para evitar usar un valor null"},{"term":"teammate","gloss":"companero del equipo"}]
duringTaskPromptEn:
Customer customer = customerRepository.findById(id).orElse(null);
emailService.sendWelcomeEmail(customer.getEmail());
duringTaskInstructionEs: Escribe en ingles un comentario de code review de 2 frases. Debe sonar amable y sugerir agregar una validacion antes de usar customer.
expectedAnswerEn: Could we add a null check before calling customer.getEmail()? If findById returns null, this line will fail and the request will crash.
postTaskLanguageFocus: {"grammar":"could we for polite suggestion","discourseMarkers":["if"],"keyCollocations":["add a null check","this line will fail","request will crash"]}
postTaskExplanationEs: En code review conviene usar preguntas suaves como Could we... para sonar colaborativo. La segunda frase explica el riesgo concreto y hace que la sugerencia se vea razonable.

titleEs: Comentar un problema de performance en useEffect
descriptionEs: Escribe una observacion tecnica sobre requests innecesarias.
preTaskContextEn: A teammate updated a React page and the UI works, but the network panel now shows repeated requests. You want to point out the issue without sounding harsh.
preTaskGlossesJson: [{"term":"dependency array","gloss":"lista que controla cuando vuelve a correr useEffect"},{"term":"network panel","gloss":"herramienta del navegador para ver solicitudes"},{"term":"repeated request","gloss":"solicitud que se ejecuta varias veces sin necesidad"}]
duringTaskInstructionEs: Escribe en ingles un comentario de code review de 2 a 4 frases. Explica el problema y sugiere una mejora.
expectedAnswerEn: I think this dependency array may trigger unnecessary requests because the effect runs again every time user changes after setUser. Could we depend only on userId here? That would fetch data when the route changes and avoid an extra request loop.
postTaskLanguageFocus: {"grammar":"hedging plus would for consequence","discourseMarkers":["because","could we","that would"],"keyCollocations":["dependency array","unnecessary requests","request loop"]}
postTaskExplanationEs: Hedging como I think reduce friccion en comentarios de revision. Tambien ayuda explicar la consecuencia con would para que la sugerencia no parezca solo una preferencia estilistica.

titleEs: Hacer un naming nit profesional
descriptionEs: Escribe un comentario breve sobre nombres poco claros.
preTaskContextEn: The implementation looks correct, but one function name is too vague for future readers. You want to leave a nit that is useful and respectful.
preTaskGlossesJson: [{"term":"nit","gloss":"comentario menor de estilo o claridad"},{"term":"future readers","gloss":"personas que leeran el codigo mas adelante"},{"term":"vague name","gloss":"nombre poco especifico"}]
duringTaskInstructionEs: Escribe en ingles un comentario de code review de 2 frases. Debe explicar por que el nombre no es claro y sugerir una alternativa.
expectedAnswerEn: Small naming nit: handleData feels a bit vague here because the function specifically builds an invoice summary. Would renameInvoiceSummary or buildInvoiceSummary be clearer for future readers?
postTaskLanguageFocus: {"grammar":"would be clearer for recommendation","discourseMarkers":["because"],"keyCollocations":["naming nit","future readers","clearer name"]}
postTaskExplanationEs: En un naming nit conviene ser breve y justificar la sugerencia con el dominio del metodo. Future readers es una forma natural de enmarcar el beneficio de un nombre mas especifico.

titleEs: Señalar una preocupacion arquitectonica
descriptionEs: Escribe un comentario sobre acoplamiento entre capas.
preTaskContextEn: A pull request adds database queries directly inside a controller. The code passes tests, but you are concerned about how this pattern could spread across the codebase.
preTaskGlossesJson: [{"term":"coupling","gloss":"dependencia fuerte entre partes del sistema"},{"term":"controller","gloss":"capa que recibe la solicitud HTTP"},{"term":"spread across the codebase","gloss":"extenderse como patron al resto del proyecto"}]
duringTaskInstructionEs: Escribe en ingles un comentario de code review de 3 o 4 frases. Debe explicar la preocupacion arquitectonica y sugerir una direccion mejor.
expectedAnswerEn: I am a bit concerned that the controller now orchestrates repository calls directly. That works for this endpoint, but it also increases coupling between the web layer and the persistence layer. Could we move this composition into an application service so the controller stays thin and the pattern remains consistent across the codebase?
postTaskLanguageFocus: {"grammar":"concerned that for architectural risk","discourseMarkers":["but","also","so"],"keyCollocations":["increase coupling","web layer","thin controller"]}
postTaskExplanationEs: Una preocupacion arquitectonica necesita explicar el riesgo sistémico, no solo el detalle local. Increase coupling, web layer y thin controller son expresiones muy naturales para este tipo de revision.

titleEs: Pedir mejor cobertura de pruebas
descriptionEs: Escribe una solicitud de pruebas para un flujo con edge cases.
preTaskContextEn: The feature introduces retry logic for webhook processing. The implementation looks reasonable, but the current tests only cover the happy path.
preTaskGlossesJson: [{"term":"edge case","gloss":"caso limite o poco comun pero importante"},{"term":"retry logic","gloss":"mecanismo para reintentar una operacion fallida"},{"term":"happy path","gloss":"escenario ideal sin errores"}]
duringTaskInstructionEs: Escribe en ingles un comentario de code review de 3 frases. Debe pedir tests adicionales y mencionar al menos dos casos que faltan.
expectedAnswerEn: Could we add tests around the retry behavior before merging this? Right now we only cover the happy path, but I would also want a case where the third attempt still fails and a case where a later retry succeeds so we can verify that markWebhookProcessed is called exactly once. Those edge cases seem important for queue safety.
postTaskLanguageFocus: {"grammar":"would also want for additional coverage","discourseMarkers":["but","so"],"keyCollocations":["retry behavior","happy path","edge cases"]}
postTaskExplanationEs: Pedir pruebas adicionales funciona mejor cuando nombras escenarios concretos y explicas el riesgo. That edge cases seem important for queue safety vincula la cobertura con la confiabilidad del sistema.

titleEs: Resumir un mini postmortem de incidente
descriptionEs: Lee un reporte corto y resume causa, impacto y accion siguiente.
preTaskContextEn: The professor wants students to read short incident reports in English. Your job is to turn the report into a simple operational summary.
preTaskGlossesJson: [{"term":"impact","gloss":"efecto del problema en usuarios o sistema"},{"term":"rollback","gloss":"volver a una version anterior estable"},{"term":"incident","gloss":"falla o interrupcion en produccion"}]
duringTaskPromptEn:
Incident summary
- Start time: 09:10 UTC
- End time: 09:34 UTC
- Impact: 27% of checkout requests returned HTTP 500
- Cause: a new cache key format produced collisions between guest carts and signed-in carts
- Mitigation: rollback to release 2026.04.07.1
duringTaskInstructionEs: Escribe en ingles 3 frases. Debes resumir el impacto, la causa y la mitigacion aplicada.
expectedAnswerEn: The incident affected checkout between 09:10 and 09:34 UTC, and 27% of requests returned HTTP 500. The root cause was a new cache key format that mixed guest carts with signed-in carts. The team mitigated the problem by rolling back to release 2026.04.07.1.
postTaskLanguageFocus: {"grammar":"past simple for incident narrative","discourseMarkers":["and","by"],"keyCollocations":["root cause","cache key format","roll back"]}
postTaskExplanationEs: Los incident reports suelen resumirse con impacto, causa y mitigacion en ese orden. Past simple y collocations como root cause ayudan a producir una narrativa tecnica clara y corta.

titleEs: Explicar un extracto de RFC interno
descriptionEs: Resume una propuesta tecnica y su justificacion principal.
preTaskContextEn: An engineering manager shared an RFC excerpt about background jobs. A new teammate asked for a quick summary before the design review meeting.
preTaskGlossesJson: [{"term":"background job","gloss":"proceso asincrono que corre fuera del request principal"},{"term":"throughput","gloss":"cantidad de trabajo procesado por unidad de tiempo"},{"term":"queue consumer","gloss":"proceso que lee y ejecuta trabajos desde una cola"}]
duringTaskPromptEn:
RFC excerpt
We propose moving PDF invoice generation out of the request-response cycle and into a queue-based worker. This change should reduce checkout latency during peak traffic and make retries explicit when rendering fails. The worker will consume jobs from invoice.render and store generated files in object storage.
duringTaskInstructionEs: Escribe en ingles un resumen de 3 o 4 frases. Debes explicar la propuesta, el beneficio principal y el nuevo flujo.
expectedAnswerEn: The RFC proposes moving PDF invoice generation from the request-response path into a queue-based worker. The main benefit is lower checkout latency during peak traffic, and retries become easier to manage when rendering fails. In the new flow, a worker consumes jobs from invoice.render and saves the generated files in object storage.
postTaskLanguageFocus: {"grammar":"proposes and main benefit for summaries","discourseMarkers":["and","in the new flow"],"keyCollocations":["request-response path","queue-based worker","object storage"]}
postTaskExplanationEs: Los resúmenes de RFC suelen partir de la propuesta y luego pasar al por que y al como. Main benefit y in the new flow te ayudan a separar esas dos capas sin perder claridad.

titleEs: Resumir un architecture decision record
descriptionEs: Explica una decision tecnica y el trade-off aceptado.
preTaskContextEn: A student is reading an ADR for the first time and does not know which parts matter most. You want to translate the formal note into a clear explanation.
preTaskGlossesJson: [{"term":"trade-off","gloss":"intercambio entre una ventaja y una desventaja"},{"term":"ADR","gloss":"registro corto de una decision de arquitectura"},{"term":"operational overhead","gloss":"trabajo adicional para operar o mantener algo"}]
duringTaskPromptEn:
ADR-014
Decision: Use PostgreSQL row-level security for tenant isolation instead of application-side WHERE tenant_id filters.
Rationale: Centralizing tenant isolation in the database lowers the risk of missing a filter in new queries.
Consequence: Query debugging becomes harder and the team accepts extra operational overhead in local development.
duringTaskInstructionEs: Escribe en ingles 3 frases. Debes resumir la decision, la razon principal y la consecuencia aceptada.
expectedAnswerEn: This ADR chooses PostgreSQL row-level security for tenant isolation instead of relying on application-side tenant_id filters. The main reason is that database-level rules reduce the risk of forgetting a filter in new queries. The trade-off is harder query debugging and some extra operational overhead during local development.
postTaskLanguageFocus: {"grammar":"chooses and the trade-off is","discourseMarkers":["instead of","the main reason","the trade-off is"],"keyCollocations":["tenant isolation","database-level rules","query debugging"]}
postTaskExplanationEs: En un ADR importa captar decision, rationale y consequence. The trade-off is es una estructura muy util para mostrar que la decision no es gratis, pero aun asi fue aceptada.

titleEs: Interpretar un benchmark de base de datos
descriptionEs: Resume resultados, comparacion y recomendacion operativa.
preTaskContextEn: The data platform team benchmarked two query plans after adding a new composite index. They want a written summary for a migration ticket.
preTaskGlossesJson: [{"term":"p95 latency","gloss":"latencia del percentil 95, usada para medir colas largas"},{"term":"query plan","gloss":"estrategia que usa la base de datos para ejecutar una consulta"},{"term":"composite index","gloss":"indice que combina varias columnas"}]
duringTaskPromptEn:
Benchmark summary
Dataset: 12.4M orders
Query: recent orders by merchant and status
Before composite index:
- p95 latency: 842ms
- rows scanned: 2.1M
After composite index (merchant_id, status, created_at desc):
- p95 latency: 118ms
- rows scanned: 34k
Recommendation: ship the index in the next maintenance window
duringTaskInstructionEs: Escribe en ingles 4 frases. Debes comparar antes y despues y cerrar con la recomendacion.
expectedAnswerEn: The new composite index improved the query significantly. Before the change, the query had 842ms p95 latency and scanned 2.1 million rows. After the index on merchant_id, status, and created_at, p95 latency dropped to 118ms and scanned rows fell to 34 thousand. Based on that improvement, the team recommends shipping the index in the next maintenance window.
postTaskLanguageFocus: {"grammar":"before after comparison","discourseMarkers":["before","after","based on"],"keyCollocations":["p95 latency","scanned rows","maintenance window"]}
postTaskExplanationEs: Los benchmarks se entienden mejor con una estructura before versus after muy explicita. Based on that improvement te permite pasar de datos a recomendacion sin sonar brusco ni subjetivo.

titleEs: Resumir un design doc de caché distribuida
descriptionEs: Explica objetivo, restricciones y decision final del diseno.
preTaskContextEn: The architecture group circulated a design doc for a new distributed cache layer. A teammate missed the meeting and asked you for a concise but accurate summary.
preTaskGlossesJson: [{"term":"cache invalidation","gloss":"proceso para eliminar datos viejos de una cache"},{"term":"stale data","gloss":"datos desactualizados que ya no representan la verdad actual"},{"term":"write-through","gloss":"patron donde la cache se actualiza al mismo tiempo que la base"}]
duringTaskPromptEn:
Design doc summary
Goal: reduce repeated reads for product pricing under high traffic.
Options considered:
1. local in-memory cache per pod
2. shared Redis cache with versioned keys
Decision: use shared Redis cache with versioned keys.
Reason: local caches are simpler but risk stale data during rapid price updates. Versioned Redis keys increase complexity, but they make cache invalidation safer across pods.
duringTaskInstructionEs: Escribe en ingles 4 o 5 frases. Debes mencionar el objetivo, las opciones evaluadas, la decision y el trade-off principal.
expectedAnswerEn: The design doc aims to reduce repeated product pricing reads under high traffic. The team considered a local in-memory cache per pod and a shared Redis cache with versioned keys. It chose the shared Redis option because local caches are simpler but more likely to serve stale data during fast price updates. The trade-off is extra complexity in exchange for safer cache invalidation across pods.
postTaskLanguageFocus: {"grammar":"aims to and in exchange for","discourseMarkers":["because","but","in exchange for"],"keyCollocations":["versioned keys","stale data","cache invalidation"]}
postTaskExplanationEs: En design docs importa resumir decision y trade-off, no repetir todo el debate. In exchange for es util a nivel B2 para expresar una ganancia obtenida al aceptar mas complejidad.
